review_text: One of the best movies ever made. Take this script, feed it to AI, and fire all the Hollywood writers trying to do action movie scripts today.

In my opinion, the greatest movie ever made! I’ve watched this over 80 times in my life and still am blown away. Never gets old and has held up over the years as complete gold. Music score by sir John Williams is just astonishing. Ford is amazing in every role he plays but this is epic. Spielberg & Lucas are genius. From the iconic hat, whip, jacket, special effects, stuntmen, cast, crew, costumes, locations to the camera work and music = pure perfection. Bravissimo!! 100% my favorite movie and hero since the beginning of time.

This is a great movie. It has great action, great cinematography and a compelling story. Harrison Ford does an amazing job in this movie. The special effects in this movie are great. The stunts are also great and the sets production made to destroy (like Marion’s bar for example look great). I love the effects for the Ark of the Covenant opening and for the face burning. I love how we see Indy travel around the world in this and interact with others.
Great film. Harrison Ford is awesome and the scenery is gorgeous.

Watched Indiana Jones Raiders of the Lost Ark this year. This golden 80s movie is one of the best. It is about an Archaeologist who is helping the government and has to find a lost treasure and Germany is trying to stop him from helping find it. The storytelling is one of a kind and the acting is awesome, a great performance from Harrison Ford. Overall I would rate this movie 5.0 stars out of 5 stars. I am ready to see what challenges are going to come in Indiana Jones’s way next time. My favorite scene is when he looks into the room and says Snakes ! Why did it have to be snakes? I would recommend this movie.

A dismal Spielberg effort, probably made with offcuts - possibly when he was tired, or high, or just feeling like a kicked dog. Many gaps in the plot, some very UNfunny shots (Including a twerp with a pipe stuck in his mouth, but absolutely no idea how to "smoke" it ..( A peculiar failure, that presented juvenile thinking, occasionally weak attempts at humor, and a rather boring piece of film. Glad he went on to better more "populist" things, with a little more sophistication.
Totally comfort movie. Extraordinary old action classic

A decently interesting plot compared to later entries in the Indiana Jones series, but disappointing compared to Spielberg's multiple masterpieces in the Science Fiction and Horror genres. This film borders on mindless action at times, and while its stunts and practical effects are well-done, they tend to lack plot importance and feel shoehorned in for the sake of pleasing audiences. The ancient tombs that Indy explores don't feel like real environments but rather unnaturally linear gauntlets of the most gross and shocking things the writers could think of. Worst of all, while Harrison Ford sells the role as expected, Indiana Jones just isn't a likeable protagonist due to his questionable morals and blatant sexism, even by 80s standards. Overall, while this film comes from an incredible director and has an interesting premise, it ultimately comes off as mindless action for the masses.
one of the best but some gore

Raiders of the Lost Ark is Spielberg at his most instinctive — a craftsman of spectacle who understands the sheer joy of movement. Every scene hums with energy and precision: the crack of the whip, the dust rising in the chases, the determined glint in Harrison Ford’s eyes. It’s cinema that lives through the body, yet somehow speaks to the soul. Indiana Jones is more than a hero; he’s the modern incarnation of the classic adventurer, marked by irony and exhaustion. Ford plays him with that rare mix of arrogance and weariness — as if the character knows glory is always fleeting. That quiet awareness makes him unforgettable. Spielberg conducts rhythm like a symphony of tension and humor — there’s something Chaplinesque in how action meets levity, and something of Howard Hawks in how everything feels effortless. The production design and John Williams’ score complete the alchemy: every chord, every shadow, reinforces the birth of a myth before our eyes. See Raiders of the Lost Ark reminds us why some films are called “classics.” Not because they age well, but because they still make us feel — wonder, laughter, danger. It’s the kind of adventure cinema rarely makes anymore, but one it never stops promising. Rate: 8,5/10

Raiders of the Lost Ark is not just an adventure film; it’s a masterclass in character-driven storytelling. Every trap-dodging, villain-punching, relic-recovering moment works not just because of Spielberg’s pacing or John Williams’s soaring score — both of which are phenomenal— but because it’s Indiana Jones facing it. He’s not just one of the best characters ever put to screen — he owns the screen.

Raiders of the Lost Ark checks all the boxes, just like Star Wars does. Perfect score, incredible cast making for memorable characters, great writing in both the plot and dialogue, and it’s just overall a very fun and wholesome adventure. It still holds up today and forever will. Harrison Ford absolutely shines in this role which makes sense because it seems to be his favorite role of his career. Karen Allen is also perfect as the love interest and she more than holds her own, even stealing the spotlight at times. Raiders of the Lost Ark is always an adventure you can sit down, watch, and enjoy.
